allow: Bash(git add app/ components/ lib/ next.config.js package.json package-lock.json postcss.config.js tailwind.config.ts tsconfig.json vercel.json .gitignore)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Initial commit: S-P Movies website

- Next.js 14 with App Router and Tailwind CSS
- Dark theme with Netflix-style design
- Movie listing page with responsive grid
- Movie detail page with Telegram/Terabox download buttons
- Admin panel with authentication \(Satyaa/Satyaa1234\)
- Full CRUD operations for movies
- API routes for movies and auth
- Vercel deployment configuration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
Bash(git config user.email "claude@anthropic.com" && git config user.name "Claude")

Bash(git add CLAUDE.md && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Add CLAUDE.md for future Claude Code instances

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git add lib/db.ts && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Fix: Use /tmp directory for Vercel serverless

- Use /tmp which is writable in Vercel serverless environment
- Add error handling for file read operations
- Add fallback for admin verification if file missing

Note: Data in /tmp is ephemeral. For persistent storage on Vercel,
consider using Vercel KV or Vercel Postgres.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Add Hugging Face dataset support for movie storage

- Replace file-based storage with Hugging Face Datasets support
- Uses REST API to load/save movies to HF Hub
- Requires HF_REPO_ID and HF_TOKEN env vars for persistence
- Falls back to sample data when not configured
- Update API routes to handle async database operations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git add CLAUDE.md && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Update CLAUDE.md for clarity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git add -A && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Fix: Auth cookie handling and credentials

- Fix login API to properly set cookie in response
- Add credentials: 'include' to all fetch calls for auth
- Update logout to explicitly clear cookie in response
- Add error alerts to admin dashboard for better feedback

The issue was that cookies weren't being sent with API requests,
causing auth to fail silently when adding/deleting movies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
